data.title: As a sprint orchestrator, I can merge worktree branches without .test-index conflicts
data.description:
## Description

**What**: Add a union merge driver for .test-index in .gitattributes so that append-only .test-index entries from parallel worktree agents auto-resolve during merge.
**Why**: .test-index conflicts are the most common merge conflict when integrating worktree agent branches. Both sides append new entries; the resolution is always "keep both." The union merge driver automates this.
**Scope**:
- IN: Add /.test-index merge=union to .gitattributes; verify union driver resolves synthetic conflicts correctly
- OUT: Changes to .test-index format or parsing logic; changes to pre-commit hooks

## Done Definitions

- When this story is complete, merging two branches that both add new .test-index entries produces a merged file containing all entries from both sides without a conflict marker
  ← Satisfies: "When two worktree branches both add entries to .test-index, the merge resolves automatically without manual conflict resolution (union merge driver)"
- When this story is complete, unit tests written and passing for all new or modified logic

## Considerations

- [Maintainability] Union driver produces duplicate lines when both sides add identical entries — cosmetic only, does not affect test gate behavior
- [Testing] Integration test should create a synthetic merge scenario with conflicting .test-index additions and verify auto-resolution

## Escalation Policy

**Escalation policy**: Proceed unless a significant assumption is required to continue — one that could send the implementation in the wrong direction. Escalate only when genuinely blocked without a reasonable inference. Document all assumptions made without escalating.
